title: Reading and Use of English – Part 1
description: For questions 1–8, read the text below and choose which answer (A, B, C or D) best completes each gap.

content.text: The dream of human flight (0) back thousands of years, but it was not until the early 20th century that it became a reality. The Wright brothers are (1) with making the first powered flight in 1903. Their achievement (2) the way for rapid developments in aviation technology. During the First World War, aircraft were (3) to use for military purposes, which accelerated their development. Commercial aviation (4) off in the 1920s, making travel across continents possible for the first time. Pilots became celebrities, and long-distance flights (5) the public's imagination. Jet engines, introduced after the Second World War, (6) about a revolution in air travel. Today, millions of passengers (7) on aircraft to travel for business and leisure. Despite concerns about environmental impact, the aviation industry continues to grow as engineers (8) on developing more sustainable technologies for the future.

example.explanation: 'Goes back' is the correct expression to indicate that something goes back in time.

questions.explanation: 'Credited with' significa reconocer a alguien por un logro.
'Paved the way for' (with 'the way for' already in the text) is the idiom to facilitate future developments.
'Put to use' (with 'to use' already in the text) means to use something for a specific purpose.
'Took off' (with 'off' already in the text) means to take off or start to succeed.
'Captured the imagination' is the right placement to fascinate the audience.
'Brought about' (with 'about' already in the text) means to cause or bring about an important change.
'Rely on' (with 'on' already in the text) is the most common expression for depending on something.
'Work on' (with 'on' already in the text) means to work on developing something.
